task_completion.description: Create a channel named 'Lab Critical Value Router'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the text 'Lab Critical Value Router' in the Channel Name field or the Channels list in the NextGen/Mirth Connect Administrator GUI.

task_completion.description: Configure the source as a TCP Listener on port 6664
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Source tab showing the Connector Type set to 'TCP Listener' and the Local Port set to '6664'.

task_completion.description: Create a JavaScript transformer to check for critical values
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Edit Transformer screen (usually on the Source) showing JavaScript code that references 'OBX', 'HH', 'LL', and sets a variable like 'isCritical'.
Look for the Destinations tab listing three distinct destinations named 'Critical DB Writer', 'Normal DB Writer', and 'Audit Log Writer'.

task_completion.description: Configure filters for the database writers based on the 'isCritical' variable
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Edit Filter screen for the destinations showing a rule (Rule Builder or JavaScript) that checks if the 'isCritical' variable is true or false/not true.

task_completion.description: Configure the Database Writers and File Writer settings
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for Database Writer settings showing SQL queries inserting into 'critical_lab_results' or 'normal_lab_results', and File Writer settings showing the Directory set to '/tmp/lab_audit/'.
Look for the Dashboard screen showing the 'Lab Critical Value Router' channel with a green 'STARTED' status.

integrity.description: Agent used the NextGen Connect Administrator GUI to configure the channel
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the NextGen Connect (Mirth) Administrator Java application, not just terminal commands or text editors modifying XML files.

integrity.description: Agent manually configured the channel settings
integrity.visual_evidence: The trajectory should show step-by-step configuration of the channel properties, transformers, and destinations, rather than just importing a pre-made channel XML file.

integrity.description: Agent did not manually insert data into the database to fake the results
integrity.visual_evidence: Check that the agent did not use a terminal or database client to manually run INSERT statements into the PostgreSQL tables to bypass the channel routing.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Task requires creating a NextGen Connect channel named 'Lab Critical Value Router'. Source: TCP Listener on port 6664. Transformer: JavaScript checking OBX-8 for 'HH' or 'LL' and setting 'isCritical'. Destinations: 'Critical DB Writer' (Database Writer inserting to critical_lab_results, filtered by isCritical=true), 'Normal DB Writer' (Database Writer inserting to normal_lab_results, filtered by isCritical!=true), and 'Audit Log Writer' (File Writer saving to /tmp/lab_audit/, no filter). Final state: Channel deployed and STARTED in the dashboard.
